faqs.question: What are the most common pantry pests in Hunts Point homes?
faqs.answer: The most common pantry pests in Hunts Point include Indian meal moths, saw-toothed grain beetles, flour beetles, and weevils. These pests infest dry goods like cereals, flour, pasta, rice, nuts, and pet food. They can enter homes through infested groceries or small cracks and quickly spread throughout kitchen cabinets if not treated promptly.

faqs.question: How much does pantry pest control cost in Hunts Point?
faqs.answer: Our pantry pest control service in Hunts Point ranges from $350 to $550, depending on the severity of the infestation and the size of your kitchen area. This includes a thorough inspection, targeted treatment of affected areas, disposal recommendations for contaminated items, and preventive measures to keep pests from returning. We provide upfront pricing with no surprise charges.

faqs.question: How long does it take to eliminate a pantry pest infestation?
faqs.answer: Most pantry pest infestations can be controlled within one treatment, but complete elimination typically takes 2-4 weeks as eggs hatch and remaining pests are exposed to treatments. We recommend thoroughly cleaning and inspecting all pantry items, discarding infested products, and storing remaining food in airtight containers. A follow-up inspection may be scheduled to ensure complete eradication.

faqs.question: Are your pantry pest treatments safe for families and food preparation areas?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products specifically designed for use in kitchens and food storage areas. Our treatments target pests while being safe for your family, pets, and food preparation surfaces when applied by our licensed technicians. We focus on non-toxic methods like pheromone traps, targeted applications in cracks and crevices, and preventive exclusion techniques to minimize chemical use in your kitchen.
